title: BAI President Sarma on Monkeys at India Open: "What is the problem?"
indian_angle: The comment is from the BAI President regarding an incident at the India Open, an Indian event.
summary: BAI President Himanta Biswa Sarma commented on the presence of monkeys at the India Open, questioning why it would be an issue if they came to watch a game.

title: Indian Shuttlers Urged to Develop Independent Thinking, Win Without Coach
indian_angle: This article directly addresses Indian shuttlers and their development, focusing on self-reliance in matches.
summary: An opinion piece suggests that Indian badminton players need to cultivate independent thinking and the ability to win matches without constant coaching support.

title: New 3x15 Scoring System to Debut in Indian Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: This is a significant rule change implemented by the BAI for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in India.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has implemented a new 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships, set to debut at Ernakulam.

title: BAI Issues Circular for Form-1 Issuance to Badminton Sportspersons
indian_angle: This is an official circular from the Badminton Association of India, directly affecting Indian badminton players.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has released a circular detailing the procedure for issuing Form-1 to badminton sportspersons.

title: Rounak Chouhan Upsets World No 6 Chou Tien Chen at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan achieved a major upset by defeating a top-ranked player at an international event.
summary: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan, a self-proclaimed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, caused a stir at the US Open by defeating World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen.

title: India's Asian Games Badminton Squad Announced, Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: This news details the Indian badminton squad for the upcoming Asian Games, including key players like Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.
summary: The Thomas Cup squad has reassembled for the Asian Games, with Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand making their return to the national team.
